author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
## Findings

Reconciled `README.md` to the v3 storage model. Four edits:

1. **Status header** (line 5): dropped the stale "178 workspace tests green" line. Test counts drift fast — we're at 587 today; the absolute number serves no reader.
2. **"What jjforge tracks" historical note** (line 51): the v1 → v2 rename paragraph used to list "bookmark" among the things renamed to "issue." Reworded to drop "bookmark" and append a one-line forward pointer noting that v3 (`bd98097`, 2026-06-24) moved storage to `refs/jjf/*` — sends the reader to the Architecture section.
3. **`jjf init` comment** (line 91): changed `# creates the `issues` bookmark` to `# plants the refs/jjf/* namespace`.
4. **Architecture section** (line 141-184): rewrote from the bookmark model to the per-issue-ref model. Lists the three ref families (`refs/jjf/issues/<id>`, `refs/jjf/memories/<key>`, `refs/jjf/meta/format-version`), describes the `git update-ref` CAS write path, calls out "git HEAD never moves" explicitly (the v3 safety property the user reporting fj#1 was probably encountering in a colocated repo), and frames sync as "`jjf push` round-trips the whole `refs/jjf/*` namespace via standard git transport." Replaced the merge-section's "merge commit with one Jjf-Op: merge trailer per resolved issue" with a per-issue framing matching v3. Appended a one-paragraph note about the v3 cutover so historical readers aren't confused.
5. **"Why jj-native"** (line 186-203): replaced the "Bookmarks are the unit of sync" bullet with "`refs/jjf/*` is the unit of sync" — same shape, correct mechanism. Also updated the change-IDs bullet to reflect v3 reality (per-issue ref stability across host-repo rewrites, not bookmark-moves-with-files).

Also fixed a stale "memories travel with the `issues` bookmark" line in `docs/quickstart.md:257` — drive-by, since it was the same class of drift.

Acceptance: `grep -in bookmark README.md` now returns only two hits, both in the v3-cutover historical pointers (lines 56 and 177).

## Recommendation

Close. The README matches `docs/quickstart.md` on the storage model. No code change; tests still 587/587.

## Confidence

high. Bookmark-claim grep is clean; the new sections cross-reference v3 commits and source-of-truth docs (storage-format.md, cli-json.md).

## Open follow-ups

- none.
